claims.summary: High entropy and lambda4+ tail energy can exceed ordinary 48D delivery ceilings and should be preserved as felt overflow instead of silently flattened.
claims.rationale: The current codec overflow report preserves pre-bound intensity, reports clipped dimensions and lane summaries, keeps live delivery bounded, and tests no-overflow silence.

claims.summary: Narrative arc compression could lose direction, curvature, or velocity even when final 48D output remains bounded.
claims.rationale: Narrative arc tests cover direction, lost-vs-finding projection, four-point coiling direction, curvature, and velocity/acceleration without enabling live gain.

claims.summary: Dynamic emotional ceilings, adaptive gain, or tail-specific nonlinear scaling might make high-entropy texture more faithful but would change live vector delivery.
claims.rationale: Existing readiness hooks are default-off; any live emotional aperture or gain change remains Tier 4/5 authority-gated because it changes semantic delivery force.
